mnemonics: U.S.B. Port: Unary is Single (one table), Binary is Both (two tables)
keyPoints: Unary operations (e.g., SELECT, PROJECT) are performed on a single table.
Binary operations (e.g., UNION, JOIN) require two tables as input.
The result of any relational operation is always another relation (table).
commonMistakes: Attempting a binary operation (like UNION) on tables with incompatible schemas.
Forgetting that PROJECT eliminates duplicate tuples in its result.
Confusing a CARTESIAN PRODUCT with a JOIN, which is a filtered product.
